name: Camp Chef Woodwind Pro 36 WiFi Pellet Grill with Sidekick Propane Burner

specs.Cooking Area: 811 sq in total (570 main + 241 upper rack)
specs.Smoke Number: 10-setting Smoke Number control — dial in smoke intensity independently
specs.Sidekick: Integrated high-heat propane burner for searing up to 900°F

whatWorks: The 10-setting Smoke Number system is Camp Chef's most compelling differentiator. Unlike most pellet grills where smoke output is tied directly to temperature, the Woodwind Pro lets you set smoke intensity independently — so you can run heavy smoke at higher temperatures, or light smoke while staying low and slow. This level of control makes a genuine difference in the final product and puts it ahead of most Traeger models at the same price.

The integrated Sidekick propane burner is a game-changer for pellet grill limitations. Pellet grills struggle to hit the high temperatures needed for a proper sear, but the Sidekick's 900°F capability lets you finish a brisket, chicken thighs, or steaks with a real crust without needing a separate appliance. It's genuinely useful rather than gimmicky.

WiFi and Bluetooth connectivity via the Camp Chef app is well-executed. You can monitor and adjust temperature, smoke level, and the Sidekick from your phone. Probe connectivity is solid and the app notifies you when target temperatures are reached — useful for overnight smokes.

The Ash Kickin' Cleanout lever is a practical quality-of-life feature that Camp Chef patented. Instead of vacuuming out ash or scooping manually, a lever drops the ash into a removable cup below the fire pot. Combined with the pellet hopper window and heavy 22 lb hopper capacity, this is a well-thought-out grill to maintain.
worthKnowing: At $1,299 CAD, this is a significant investment and sits at the top of the mid-range pellet grill market. The Traeger Ironwood 885 is a direct competitor at the same price — the Camp Chef wins on smoke control and sear capability, the Traeger wins on software polish and brand ecosystem. Choose based on which features matter most to you.

Pellet grills require pellet management that gas and charcoal grills don't. You'll need to keep bags of quality cooking pellets on hand, protect them from moisture, and purge the hopper if changing flavors. Budget $20–30 CAD per 20 lb bag for quality hardwood pellets, which you'll go through at a rate of 1–3 lbs per hour depending on temperature.

The Sidekick propane burner requires a separate small propane canister — it does not share the same propane line as your main fuel. This is an easy thing to forget to stock before a cook. Keep a spare 1 lb propane cylinder on hand.

Cold Canadian winters affect pellet grill performance noticeably. Below -10°C, the grill struggles to reach and maintain high temperatures without using significantly more pellets. Camp Chef sells a thermal blanket accessory that helps considerably if you grill year-round.

commonComplaints.title: App connectivity drops occasionally
commonComplaints.body: Several users report intermittent WiFi drops requiring the app to be restarted. The grill continues cooking correctly — temperature control is hardware-based — but remote monitoring is lost until you reconnect.

commonComplaints.title: Sidekick adds complexity to setup
commonComplaints.body: The Sidekick propane connection and its separate canister requirement adds a setup step that some buyers find fiddly. A few reviewers wish it shared the main propane line instead of requiring its own canister.

commonComplaints.title: Smoke Number varies with weather conditions
commonComplaints.body: The actual smoke output at a given Smoke Number setting varies depending on outdoor temperature and wind. Some users find they need to recalibrate their preferences seasonally, which takes experimentation.

commonComplaints.title: Assembly takes 2+ hours
commonComplaints.body: This is a complex grill to assemble and several buyers report the manual could be clearer on the Sidekick attachment steps. Two people are strongly recommended for the assembly process.

verdict: The Camp Chef Woodwind Pro 36 is the best all-in-one pellet grill for serious outdoor cooks who want genuine smoke control flexibility and the ability to properly sear without a second appliance. The independent Smoke Number system and Sidekick burner genuinely earn their price premium over simpler pellet grills. At $1,299 CAD it's a significant buy, but buyers who use it regularly will find it's the last grill they need for years. It's ideal for enthusiasts who want to smoke, roast, bake, and sear all from one unit.

realBuyers.quote: The Smoke Number control is incredible. I can dial in exactly how smoky I want my food without sacrificing temperature control. Nothing else I've used gives you this much precision.
The Sidekick gets screaming hot in minutes. I reverse-sear my steaks on the pellet side then finish them on the Sidekick — restaurant-quality crust every time.
Monitored an overnight brisket from my phone. Temperature held within 5 degrees all night and the app notifications woke me up when the internal temp hit target. This is the future of BBQ.
Ash cleanout is 30 seconds. After every cook I pull the lever, empty the cup, and done. My old smoker took 20 minutes to clean out.

metaDescription: Camp Chef Woodwind Pro 36 review — WiFi pellet grill with Sidekick sear burner and 10-level smoke control. Worth $1,299 CAD on Amazon.ca?
introText: The Camp Chef Woodwind Pro 36 (ASIN: B09XGCFNKW) is a premium WiFi pellet grill available on Amazon.ca for $1,299 CAD. It combines a 10-setting smoke intensity control with an integrated propane Sidekick sear burner — a combination no other pellet grill at this price matches.
